description:
You are working as a database administrator inside a Linux shell at /home/user.  
Inside /home/user/db/ there is currently one SQL file named  
    improved_query.sql  

Your goal is to do the following in a single, *one-liner* terminal command (chaining with “;” or “&&” is allowed, but it must be sent as one command invocation):

1. Create a new sub-directory called  
       /home/user/db/archive_optimized_queries  
   if it does not already exist.

2. Move the file improved_query.sql from /home/user/db/ into that new directory.

3. Record the move in the text file  
       /home/user/db/move.log  
   appending exactly one line whose contents are precisely (including the final newline character):

       improved_query.sql => archive_optimized_queries

   • No leading or trailing spaces.  
   • No timestamp, no extra text.  
   • The line must end with a single “\n”.  
   • If move.log already exists, simply append this line to the end.

Acceptance checks performed by the autograder:

A. /home/user/db/archive_optimized_queries/ exists and is a directory.  
B. /home/user/db/archive_optimized_queries/improved_query.sql exists.  
C. /home/user/db/improved_query.sql does NOT exist anymore.  
D. /home/user/db/move.log exists and its very last line is exactly  
      improved_query.sql => archive_optimized_queries  

Only the last line is verified, so earlier contents (if any) are ignored.

Reminder: issue everything in **one** terminal command line. No additional manual edits are allowed after that single command runs.
truth:

Desired final state for automated verification:

2. File /home/user/db/archive_optimized_queries/improved_query.sql exists; its contents exactly match the original /home/user/db/improved_query.sql file (byte-for-byte).
3. No file named /home/user/db/improved_query.sql remains in /home/user/db/.
4. File /home/user/db/move.log exists.  
   Its last line (after stripping the terminating newline) equals the literal string:  
       improved_query.sql => archive_optimized_queries
   There must be exactly one “\n” after that line; no extra whitespace.

The autograder will inspect only the conditions listed above.
